info.name: Car Rental API
info.description:
API collection for the Car Rental project. Import this file in Postman to test all endpoints.

## Authentication (when you need a token)

| Endpoint pattern | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` |
| --- | --- |
| `POST /api/login` | **Not used** — public. Response includes `data.token`; Postman saves it to `{{token}}`. |
| `POST /api/v1/users` (register) | **Not used** — public, but the server may return **403** if registration is disabled (`allow_user_registration`). |
| `GET /api/user`, `POST /api/logout` | **Required** — Sanctum. |
| All other `/api/v1/...` routes | **Required** — same Bearer token as above. |

This collection sets **Bearer Token** auth at the collection level (`{{token}}`). Requests that must be public override auth to **No Auth** (Login, Register User).

## Setup
1. Set `base_url` (default: `http://localhost` — add port if needed, e.g. `http://localhost:8000`).
2. Run **Login**; token is stored automatically.
3. Fill path variables from responses: `car_id`, `driver_id`, `customer_id`, `transaction_id`, `booking_id`, `car_import_id`, `driver_import_id`, `bill_payment_id` (UUIDs).

## Optional query keys (frontend reference)
Many `GET` endpoints accept optional keys documented on each request: pagination (`per_page`), nested `filter[...]`, `sort`, `include`, date ranges, etc. If a key is omitted, the API uses defaults described on that request.

**User registration:** `POST /api/v1/users` needs `name`, `email`, `password`, `password_confirmation` — no Bearer; may still fail with 403 when registration is off.

**CSV imports:** `multipart/form-data` with field `file`; then poll import status by ID. Sample car CSV: `samples/cars-import.csv`. Columns: `type`, `door`, `seats`, `year`, `color`, `make`, `model`, `plate_number` (`door` and `seats` are integers).

**Transactions:** create/update bodies require `name` (max 255) where applicable. Optional `has_bill` query (`0`, `1`, `true`, `false`) on list transaction endpoints filters by whether a bill exists. **Bookings:** `GET /api/v1/bookings` lists all bookings for the authenticated user (paginated, optional `status`: `completed`, `today`, `ongoing`, `incoming`). Per-transaction `GET .../bookings` uses `status` (`upcoming`/`previous`), `period`, `car_id`, `driver_id`; newest first (`created_at`, then `start_date`).

**Bills:** `billNumber` and `invoiceNumber` are assigned automatically on create (format `INV-YYMM#####` per month). Do not send them in the request body. Bill statuses: `draft`, `issued`, `partially_paid`, `paid`, `cancelled`. Bill JSON attributes include `amountPaid` (sum of recorded payments) and `remainingBalance` (`amount` minus `amountPaid`, never below 0).

**Billing summary:** `GET /api/v1/billing/summary` returns `totalPaid` (sum of fully paid bill amounts) and `totalUnpaid` (outstanding balance on `issued` and `partially_paid` bills) for the authenticated rental company.

**Bill Payments (installments):** `POST .../bill/payments` records a single payment against a bill (multipart/form-data: `amount`, `method`, `reference_number`, `proof_image` required; `notes` optional). Accepted `method` values: `bank_transfer`, `cash`, `gcash`. Payment is only allowed when bill status is `issued` or `partially_paid`. The bill status is recomputed automatically after each payment or deletion: `issued` → `partially_paid` (partial) → `paid` (fully paid). Deleting a payment reverses the computation.

**User roles:** every user has a `role` of `admin` or `user`. Admin users can update any driver record and manage the driver-user link.

**Driver-user link:** a driver can be optionally linked to a user account via `user_id` (set by admins only via Add/Update Driver). The linked user can update their own driver record but cannot change the `user_id`. Response attribute `userId` is `null` when no user is linked.

request.description: Public endpoint — **do not** send `Authorization`. Send JSON `email` and `password`. On success, use `data.token` as `Bearer` for all `/api/v1/*` routes (except register). Postman saves `token` automatically.
**Bearer required.** Revokes the current Sanctum token. After this, set a new token via Login.
**Bearer required.** Returns the authenticated user for the current token.

Register a new user account. **No Bearer token** — this route is public. Body: `name`, `email`, `password`, `password_confirmation`. Returns **403** when registration is disabled server-side (`allow_user_registration`). When enabled, returns success without needing any API key beyond optional rate limits.

**Bearer required.** Create a car. Required body fields: `type`, `door` (integer), `seats` (integer), `year` (integer), `color`, `make`, `model`, `plate_number` (unique). Response attributes use camelCase (`plateNumber`, etc.).

Queue a CSV import of cars (multipart/form-data, `file` required).

**CSV header row (required):**
`type,door,seats,year,color,make,model,plate_number`

**Example row:**
`Sedan,4,5,2022,Black,Toyota,Camry,ABC-1234`

`door` and `seats` must be integers. `plate_number` must be unique across the database and within the file.
Fetch status/result summary for a queued car import job.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. Filter by vehicle type (string).
Optional. Filter by door count (integer, min 1).
Optional. Filter by seat count (integer, min 1).
Optional. Filter by year (integer).
Optional. Filter by color (string).
Optional. Filter by make (string).
Optional. Filter by model (string).
Optional. Filter by plate number (string).

request.description: **Bearer required.** Lists available cars. All query keys are optional; omit them to list with defaults. Response attributes: `type`, `door`, `seats`, `year`, `color`, `make`, `model`, `plateNumber`.
**Bearer required.** Returns one car. Attributes: `type`, `door`, `seats`, `year`, `color`, `make`, `model`, `plateNumber`, `createdAt`.
**Bearer required.** Partial update supported (`sometimes` rules). All fields optional: `type`, `door`, `seats`, `year`, `color`, `make`, `model`, `plate_number` (unique except current car).

**Bearer required.** Create a driver. Required: `first_name`, `last_name`, `license_number` (unique), `license_expiry_date`, `address`, `phone_number`. Optional (admin only): `user_id` (integer, must be unique across drivers — links this driver record to a user account).

Queue a CSV import of drivers (multipart/form-data, `file` required).

**CSV header row (required):**
`first_name,last_name,license_number,license_expiry_date,address,phone_number`

**Example row:**
`John,Doe,DL-12345678,2027-12-31,123 Main St,+1234567890`

`license_number` must be unique across the database and within the file.
Fetch status/result summary for a queued driver import job.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. Search string (max 100) applied to driver listing.
Optional only if `end_date` is also omitted. If you use availability mode, **both** `start_date` and `end_date` are required (`end_date` must be on or after `start_date`).
Optional only if `start_date` is also omitted. **Required together with `start_date`** for drivers available in that period.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Default: paginated all drivers. **Date range mode:** when both `start_date` and `end_date` are sent, returns drivers available in that window (validated as a pair).
**Bearer required.** Returns one driver. Attributes: `firstName`, `lastName`, `licenseNumber`, `licenseExpiryDate`, `address`, `phoneNumber`, `userId` (null if not linked to a user account).

**Bearer required.** Partial update (`sometimes` rules). All fields optional: `first_name`, `last_name`, `license_number` (unique except current driver), `license_expiry_date`, `address`, `phone_number`. **Authorization:** admin users can update any driver; a non-admin user linked to this driver (via `user_id`) can update their own record. All others receive 403. **Admin-only field:** `user_id` (nullable integer) — links or unlinks the driver to a user account; silently ignored for non-admin callers.

item.name: Billing & bills
item.description: All routes here require **Bearer** auth. Query keys below are optional unless noted.

query.description: Optional. UUID of a customer; scopes summary to that customer.
Optional. Limits `totalUnpaid` to bills issued on or before this date.
Optional. Use with filter[paid_at][to]; limits `totalPaid` to bills paid on or after this date. `paid_at` to must be on or after from.
Optional. Limits `totalPaid` to bills paid on or before this date.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Aggregated billing for the authenticated user. Response `data.attributes`: `totalPaid` (integer, sum of amounts on `paid` bills) and `totalUnpaid` (integer, outstanding balance on `issued` and `partially_paid` bills). All `filter[...]` query keys are optional; omit `filter` entirely for an unfiltered summary.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. One of: issued_at, -issued_at, created_at, -created_at.
Optional. If set to `transaction` (comma-separated list allowed but only this token is valid), response adds `included` with related transactions.
Optional. Comma-separated: draft, issued, partially_paid, paid, cancelled.
Optional. Partial match against the bill invoice number, for example `INV-260500123`.
Optional. Use with filter[issued_at][to].
Optional. Must be on or after from when both set.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Paginated bills for the current user across all customers. Response attributes include `billNumber`, `invoiceNumber`, `amount`, `amountPaid`, `remainingBalance`, `status`, `issuedAt`, `dueAt`, `paidAt`. `amountPaid` is the sum of recorded payments; `remainingBalance` is `amount` minus `amountPaid` (never below 0). Supports partial invoice lookup with `filter[invoice_number]`.
**Bearer required.** Create a customer. Required: `name`, `type` (`personal` or `business`).

query.description: Optional: 1-100

request.description: **Bearer required.** Create a transaction for the customer in the URL. Body must include `name` (string, max 255).

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. `0`, `1`, `true`, or `false` — filter by whether the transaction has a bill.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Paginated transactions for `{{customer_id}}`. Optional `has_bill` filters by bill presence.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. issued_at, -issued_at, created_at, -created_at.
Optional. Use `transaction` to populate JSON:API `included` with the bill's transaction.
Optional. Comma-separated bill statuses: draft, issued, partially_paid, paid, cancelled.
Optional. Partial match against the bill invoice number, for example `INV-260500321`.
Optional. Pair with filter[issued_at][to].
Optional. Must be on or after from when both are set.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Bills for `{{customer_id}}` belonging to the authenticated user. Same optional query keys and response attributes as **List bills (all)** (`amountPaid`, `remainingBalance`, etc.), including partial `filter[invoice_number]` search, but scoped to this customer.
Requires `customer_id` (UUID) and `name` (string, max 255).
**Bearer required.** Requires `customer_id` (UUID) and `name` (string, max 255).

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional. `0`, `1`, `true`, or `false` — filter by whether the transaction has a bill.

request.description: **Bearer required.** Paginated transactions for the authenticated user. Optional `has_bill` filters by bill presence.
Returns transaction attributes including `name`, `userId`, `customerId`, and nested `bookings` (newest first) plus `included` booking details when present.

**Bearer required.** Create a bill for a transaction. Only one bill per transaction. Body: required `amount` (integer, min 1); optional `notes`, `due_at`. Response includes auto-generated `billNumber` and `invoiceNumber` (`INV-YYMM#####`, incremented per month), plus `amountPaid` (0 on create) and `remainingBalance` (equals `amount` on create).

**Bearer required.** Returns the bill for the transaction. Attributes include `billNumber`, `invoiceNumber`, `amount`, `amountPaid`, `remainingBalance`, `status`, `notes`, `issuedAt`, `dueAt`, `paidAt`.
**Bearer required.** Returns an invoice-shaped payload for the transaction bill including `transaction`, optional `customer`, and `bookings` with embedded `car` and `driver` data.

response.name: 200 OK (example)

request.description: **Bearer required.** Partial update. Optional: `status` (`issued`, `cancelled`), `amount`, `notes`, `due_at`. Manual status transitions via this endpoint: draft→issued/cancelled, issued→cancelled. The `partially_paid` and `paid` statuses are set automatically by the payment system — do not set them manually. `billNumber` and `invoiceNumber` are read-only.
Only draft bills can be deleted.

formdata.description: Required. Integer, min 1. Must not exceed the bill's remaining balance.
Required. One of: bank_transfer, cash, gcash.
Required. Image file (jpg, jpeg, png, webp), max 10 MB.

request.description:
**Bearer required.** Record a payment (installment) against the transaction's bill. The request must be `multipart/form-data`.

**Required fields:** `amount` (integer, min 1 — must not exceed remaining balance), `method` (`bank_transfer` | `cash` | `gcash`), `reference_number` (string, max 255), `proof_image` (image: jpg/jpeg/png/webp, max 10 MB).

**Optional:** `notes`.

Only allowed when the bill status is `issued` or `partially_paid`. After recording, the bill status is recomputed automatically: partial payment → `partially_paid`; full payment → `paid`. Response attributes: `amount`, `method`, `referenceNumber`, `notes`, `proofImageUrl`, `paidAt`, `createdAt`.

**Bearer required.** Returns all payments recorded against the transaction's bill, ordered by `paid_at` descending. Response attributes per item: `amount`, `method`, `referenceNumber`, `notes`, `proofImageUrl`, `paidAt`, `createdAt`.
**Bearer required.** Deletes a payment record. After deletion, the bill status is recomputed automatically (e.g. `paid` may revert to `partially_paid` or `issued`). Returns 204 No Content.

query.description: Optional. 1–100, default 15.
Optional: completed, today, ongoing, or incoming

request.description: **Bearer required.** Paginated bookings across all of the authenticated user's transactions. Ordered by `start_date` descending. Optional `status`: `completed` (end_date < now), `today` (start_date is today), `ongoing` (start_date <= now and end_date >= now), `incoming` (start_date > now).

query.description: Bookings where end_date is before now

request.description: **Bearer required.** Bookings that have ended (end_date < now).

query.description: Bookings whose start_date falls on today

request.description: **Bearer required.** Bookings scheduled to start today.

query.description: Bookings in progress (start_date <= now and end_date >= now)

request.description: **Bearer required.** Bookings currently in progress.

query.description: Bookings that have not started yet (start_date > now)

request.description: **Bearer required.** Upcoming bookings that have not started yet.
**Bearer required.** Create a booking. Required: `car_id`, `driver_id`, `price` (integer, min 0), `start_date`, `end_date` (≥ start_date). Optional: `note`.

query.description: Optional: 1-100
Optional: upcoming or previous
Optional: week or month
Optional: car UUID
Optional: driver UUID

request.description: Bookings are ordered newest first (`created_at` desc, then `start_date` desc). Supported filters: `status` (`upcoming` or `previous`), `period` (`week` or `month`), `car_id`, `driver_id`.

query.description: Required: 'car' or 'driver'
Required: Start date
Required: End date (must be after start)

request.description: **Bearer required.** `type`, `start`, and `end` are required query parameters.
